rag_rules: Fix latched expression diagnostics by ensuring they appear only in component implementations and reference valid input ports.
Resolve type mismatch errors by aligning expression types with expected boolean or other valid AADL types.
Address 'mismatched input' and '<error>' type diagnostics by correcting syntax and ensuring all identifiers are properly resolved.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE contracts to prevent unresolved reference errors.
Ensure AGREE scalar types match AADL conventions (int, real, bool) to avoid resolution failures.
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes and type-level contracts in type scopes to avoid scoping violations.
When diagnostics point to invalid identifiers or types, prefer removing problematic clauses over introducing new architecture.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must reside in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks within appropriate component scopes.
Binary expressions in AGREE must have matching operand types; e.g., 'and' requires boolean operands.
Latched expressions are restricted to input data ports or event expressions over input event data ports.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to fix unresolved identifiers by adding new package or data type references; instead, use only existing visible identifiers.
Avoid placing implementation-level guarantees or assignments in component type annexes; move them to implementation scopes if needed.
